strengths: ~19 years of hands-on engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency systems (meets the 15-year experience bar)
Entrepreneurial track record: founded and still runs GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform used by Green Map
Recent hands-on AI-agent work at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling
concerns: No director/principal-level scope: career titles top out at Senior/Staff IC with no evidence of org-wide technical strategy or engineering leadership
AI experience is shallow and recent (~1 year of tooling work); no demonstrated AI products at scale, publications, or recognized AI expertise
No track record advising strategic customers or partners, and no cloud platform strategy work (AWS CDK usage is implementation-level, not strategic)

key_factors.factor: Seniority and leadership scope gap vs director-level IC role
Limited depth and recency of AI expertise
Experience length, breadth, and founder/startup background

response.justification: The role's core competencies are recognized AI expertise, cloud/AI platform strategy, director-level technical leadership, and customer/partner advisory. The candidate clears the minimums: a BS in Computer Science and roughly 19 years of engineering experience spanning full-stack, embedded, and high-throughput systems (e.g., low-latency micro-services at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second). Founding GISCollective satisfies the entrepreneurial preference, and current MCP/agentic-tooling work at re:cinq shows AI fluency. However, the resume shows no principal- or director-level scope — roles are Senior/Staff IC delivery positions — no recognized AI products or platforms, no advanced AI degree, and no strategic advisory experience with customers or executives, which are the defining competencies here. Strong senior engineer, but the evidence does not support a director-level AI strategy hire.
